reviews.review_msg: 3 hour lectures are mandatory, most classes are group work, bi-weekly quizzes. Assignments marked subjectively, deducts marks for incorrect citations. I would expect 0 for citation and it was a little surprising that they deduct marks that you've earned on other parts because citations has a minor mistake. Best to take 303 with other profs.

This is the hardest class I have taken at SFU so far. Grading is extremely unclear. Instructions for assignments are often hidden in lecture slides. All of the grading seems subjective and sometimes contradictory. While she is kind, she'll have you leaving every class frustrated and utterly confused. Choose another prof. Don't take this class.

She's passionate about this course... but her grading expectations are not what you expect and no one gets 100% on anything besides the quizzes. Hope that you get a good group because the averages for the group assignments are already super low.
You can tell she is passionate about the class through her lectures and the structure makes 3 hour lectures fly by

You are graded by many assignments and in-class work (no exams). Her feedback can be vague but she does outline what she expects, so you need to pay attention to what she says cause many don't. She doesn't post lecture slides which forced me to take notes (I found it helpful though). She seems to care for her students and will help you if you ask.

90% of the work you do will be in class, such as quizzes, assignments, activities in your group that are made by random at the beginning of the semester. I liked it this way because it meant less homework and studying outside of class. Overall she cares about your learning and it's not hard to beat the curve if you do your readings and participate

Kathleens 303 course was frustrating due to subjective grading, unclear criteria, and inconsistent evaluation. Excessive readings and content left little room for meaningful engagement or reflection. Creative solutions were dismissed with comments like, “This isn't Disneyland,” discouraging critical thinking. Overall, the course felt discouraging.

Although Kathleen's lectures are engaging and the content is good, her grading and the overall work structure for her class is terrible. I've never seen a class where work is so subjectively graded. All her feedback along with her TA's is very opinionated, far from objective, and at times even contradictory.

Kathleen is a really good professor, she cares about you and gives you lots of practice before assessments. Her feedback is always detailed, and she explains her grading well. If you attend all classes and pay attention to her feedback, you will do well.

This class should be taken off from the reqs. It is literally bus272, just more group work. So repetitive, and purposeless. She was so boring but was quite unfair with marks. Did not highlight what she expected from you. I truly think that this class should not be paid for, and not worth the time.

I understand that mandatory attendance and heavy reliance on group projects may just not be for some people. But if you can handle it, take 303 with Kathleen. She is clearly very passionate on what she teaches, makes sure everyone gets more than enough feedback for every assignment. She gives you all the tools to succeed, you just gotta show up.

Kathleen's course is primarily in-class, with little homework and no slide postings. While she provides engaging lectures with group activities, her grading is tough and includes feedback on all submissions. However, her teaching style may not be suitable for international students or those who have difficulty focusing in class.

You can tell she likes teaching this course but slides should have been uploaded on canvas regardless. But many people won't come for this class if the slides are uploaded and this class has a A LOT of group work. Groups won't work without people so I understand why she doesn't upload the slides. Good prof, weird course. Ethics are imp but not fun

A lot of group work and all assignments are written in class. She didn't upload any slides, so you must copy each slide yourself and do a lot of readings every week. If you don't like working with a group or are not good at writing, never take this course as you would work in a group for 70% of class time. Kathleen is nice but the course is tough.

Kathleen is nice but her BUS303 is horrible. It takes you 2/3 of class time to discuss with team members each class. Kathleen never posts any slides so you should take all notes by yourself. All assignments are in-class and must be hand-writing, and a lot of reading every week. If you don't like teamwork or are not good at writing, never take it.
I didn't learn anything from this course. The grading was extremely tough. How can ethics be so technical? I will never apply a single concept from this class in real life.

Kathleen is very nice however I didn't agree with the way she taught business ethics. The class was very lecture-heavy with some teamwork. She was a very tough grader considering this is a business ethics class which shouldn't have been. Didn't learn much from this class.

Honestly Kathleen formatted this course in the best possible way. I highly recommend taking this class with her. It is extremely stress free and you actually learn in-class with minimal outside homework. I think the other reviews were negative due to the online format of the course. I think since then she has made some major adaptations.

Kathleen is a great prof! You learn by experience in her class. She rarely has work outside class, just simple weekly readings. Theres no tests so you can just focus on reading the (free!) textbook readings to allow yourself to actually learn and grow. Did horribly on the team stakeholder assignment and still pulled through with an A.
Not much work outside of lecture. Very passionate about course content

I didn't learn anything. It was more effective trying to write what they wanted to hear than to learn. Don't have a different opinion and don't use logic in your assignments and you'll do well. Also, she expects us to remember (i.e., tests) the small stuff like specific to the story detail rather than the big picture take away or moral of the story

I wish I went to see her in office hours more, because Kathleen is extremely supportive, approachable, helpful and she gives excellent explanations if something is unclear. The course isn't difficult in course load, but the midterms, and quizzes are tricky, so you must do the readings carefully. She doesn't post slides.

Kathleen is super approachable and passionate about business ethics. She explains concepts well with lots of examples, and provides many opportunities for practice before in-class group assignments. Also, she had a solid plan to alter the class requirements as we transitioned to online learning during the pandemic, alleviating stress.

Kathleen Burke is an amazing prof and business ethics is a super important class for all business students. She provides a super encouraging and welcoming environment to participate in class discussion to explore the ethical situations that business students may face in the future. Love her.

I honestly enjoyed BUS 303 with Kathleen. The readings were relatable and interesting for the course. Kathleen captivates you with a lot of enthusiasm in her lecture. You can't blame Kathleen for having a bad team. She is super approachable and helpful, so you can discuss any of your concerns.

Ridiculous class. Grading schemes are unfair and there is a lot of weight put on team-based work (good luck if you get a bad team). Kathleen tries to make this class as serious as possible and puts up an unrealistically high expectation on her students. Expect strict grading, many assignments and difficult projects!

Class is terrible, but Kathleen makes it much better. She is extremely passionate and provides lots of help to students. She gives 2 practice sessions before the stakeholder analysis as well as gives small ungraded assignments to make everyone comfortable at the midterm. She is a really nice lady

The most useless business class you will ever take at SFU. She does not give clear instructions or expectations for assignments and marks are completely subjective. The extent of her teaching is putting up quotes on lecture slides. You can show up to every class, do all the readings an still not know what it is exactly you are learning about.
Got in to a team everyone trash talked a lot and since I did not talk much, I did not have high grades on participation. If you dont like discussion then dont take this prof. Got an F with 59% (

Taking BUS 303 with Kathleen has been the best decision of my undergrad. I loved taking this class with her- she made lectures go by so fast. I'd recommend taking this class with her. Easy A if you show up and participate in class

Found this class challenging but not in terms of the material taught. The grading scheme is relatively vague and marking on the speak up quizzes seemed subjective. That being said if you can ace the midterm, the presentation and the reflection you should get a good grade even if you're stuck with a bad group.

Best professor I have had in university. Incredibly caring, knowledgeable, receptive and passionate. ALl in all a great teacher. Group assignments and presentation makes up a lot of your grade. I had a phenomenal group that was social as well as high performing. I had a blast going to class and participating. know all the readings!! Have fun!!
Still merely a  business decision, sorry.
It is strict on mark  and unclear instruction on how to work on the assignment and exam .
Nice lady, horrible course. I wish I had dropped it when I could of.

One of those courses that makes you question why you are getting a business degree. Horrible class structure. Participation is marked so you have a lot of students expressing ridiculous and out-of-context opinions. Excruciatingly boring course and feels very expensive! However, Kathleen herself seems like a nice lady and tries her best.

The most disgusting course I've had the displeasure of taking. Dreaded attending that class everytime. This course should not be mandatory under any regard. It's ridiculous we have to take such a mundane course. I can firmly say it's a 9/10 ratio of students who hate this course and learn nothing from it. Thankfully the nightmare is over. Thank god

Excellent lecturer, her classes were always interesting. Workload was totally manageable (Intersession), grading was fair. Doesn't provide copies of slides, doesn't record lectures and forbids recording/photographing, so be prepared for a lot of writing as the slide content is important for assignments and the exam. Lecture gives essentials to pass

Kathleen is a hit or miss professor. For me at least, it was definitely a miss. A big strike out. I literally had depression going to her class, but she is passionate about ethics and if you're keen on short stories and immersing yourself in the world of ethics, she is your best bet. Preparing in advance is the best advice I can give. God bless.

Kathleen is an awesome prof. I lucked out with an amazing group, and my grade seemed to reflect that. Midterm was pretty ambiguous, as she expects you to apply knowledge to scenarios versus simply answering a right/wrong answer.  Answer a question each class and youre in the clear! Would definitely take it with her again.

Attending class is mandatory as participation is important for your mark. This class also relies a ton on your group, so hope that you get assigned to a group that is strong. Take notes in class as she doesn't post lecture slides. Mid-term was easy and the class was overall fun as it required a different level of problem solving thinking.

I admire Kathleen's passion in business ethics. You'll be reading fictions and watching some videos for homework, which can sometimes be long. I recommend reading them before class, so you can quickly review them for the midterm. Participation and teamwork matters, so be engaged in class. Best part: NO Final! I'm enjoying this class with Kathleen!

Quite possibly the easiest business class ever taken. There's 2 *IN-CLASS* group projects, yes, in-class, meaning no hw for either of them. 1 paper reflecting on your performance of one of the projects. 1 exam. Simply based on her lecture notes and examples. Participate. Very easy course. Makes a very dry subject atleast half-interesting.

Kathleen's course was fair, I didn't do most of the readings and hardly took notes in class. However, I showed up, listened and participated in asking questions and answering her questions. I may also have lucked out with a pretty good group as well so your group definitely matters. Oh ya I also went to mexico mid term and still came out with A-

This class felt quite preachy. Kathleen will put a number of irrelevant quotes on the board and expect you to be inspired. She is quite passionate about ethics, but there is not much course content that is applicable for students in business. The marking scheme is also quite vague and subjective in terms of what is a right or wrong response.

Attending class is extremely mandatory. You won't be provided notes other than the ones you take. Her exams are easy ONLY if you review your class notes. Going back to class notes she tells you in lecture what to write, if you can go home and review the notes for exams expect yourself to get a good mark. She's an amazing professor.

This course has valuable takeaway information. However, Kathleen's class requires working with a team of 4-6 members throughout the entire course. The midterm is tricky. To do well you must come to lectures and takes notes in class. Follow her specific grading criteria, participate in class, and do well on the final paper to do well.

She has lots of material needed to be read which is useless, caz she only test very small part of them. She speaks so fast during class, and sometimes you even do not know what's going on. Her midterm is kind of ridiculous, and you have no idea about how to prepare for the exams. DO NOT TAKE HER COURSE.
This course was ok. It was super hard to know what was expected of you AND way too much of your mark relies on your group in class The work load is manageable though.
Kathleen is a nice professor, but she does not have clear instructions on what she expects from the assignments which can be a big problem. Her TA is willing to help students but grades very harshly.

There is no lecture slides.But she speaks fast and you can't make notes immediately. There are a lot of assignments that i think are useless. If you want to get a good grade, avoid her or you will get sadness.

Kathleen is a great prof. She is  genuinely interested in ethics and the team based group work is awesome. Kathleen likes to challenge her students and lots of class questions are encouraged. Assignments and exams were fair, but make sure you stay up to date on the material or else this class will creep up on you very quick.

Don't take business ethics if you don't need it to graduate. This class is a joke and is completely useless. What we learn in this class has absolutely no correlation with the real world. Are you really going to use ethical theories for making decisions? NO.. you're going to do what you feel is right.
Not clear what she expects in the assignments and presentations. Unstructured lecture.

extremely humble and engaging prof. best business professor i've had so far. as long as you do the readings (book is very engaging) you will do great in this class. a lot of group work which really builds your interpersonal skills. great teacher
Great class.  She really promotes critical thinking.

Best prof ever. She is super poetic and engaging, and even if I hadn't gotten a decent mark in the class, I wouldn't have minded. What I got out of it was useful towards building character. The way her energy takes command of the room while she lectures is impressive. I wish we had more profs like her.

Hated this class.Class average is too high. Her lecture notes are only a few words. She tends to speak fast that it was impossible to write down the lecture notes and listen to her at the same time. Makes us read hundreds of pages of worthless material, which barely gets mentioned on the midterm. If you care about your grades, stay away from her.

Class average for all assignments and midterms are unusually high. This is a nightmare for any business student. I predict that the class average is 78% based on all the assignments. And the presentation is marked by other business students. Reflections are impossible to get perfect and assignment instructions are vague. She cut 30 min on the mt.

Kathleen is a teriffic professor. What I expected to be a boring fluff class was anything but. She is very engaging, supportive and friendly. Very few teachers have impacted me to the extent that she has.

Very bad class. She gives only 75 min to complete the exam. This class is hard and requires you to think quickly and critically. Honestly, you don't even need to read the textbook because 70% of the test requires your to analyze a case. And to make this class worse, she assigns an 8 member group project which gets marked by your peers.
Best prof I've had at SFU by far... very motivating and she makes a big effort to get to know her students - I would recommend her Ethics class to anyone

Amazing prof! Logic and arguments are very compelling. Requires you to think critically on your feet. Lectures are long but very informative. Topics can be dry but she tries her best to make it interesting with relatable stories. Very useful words and very practical course.
SHE IS AMAZING! The lecture was interesting and helpful, and midterm was based on lecture notes.You will do very well if you attend every lecture. I love this woman.
TAKING 303 WITH HIM IS HIGHLY RECOMMENDED. EXAMS ARE STRAIGHTFORWARD AND TOTALLY BASED ON THE SLIDES. CLASS DISCUSSION IS FASCINATING.

AWESOME prof. He's so knowledgeable and is really good at initiating discussion. I loved going to lecture every week. Textbook is useless. Tests are based on powerpoints. Be really thorough when answering test questions. Class work seems easy enough but he can be picky with marking. One presentation and case study with group.
The class is fun. He shows a lot of videos. He doesn't impose but wants you to think and take responsibility. He definitely knows his stuff and can deliver it in clear manner.

He's nice and funny but all he did is to read from the powerpoint slides and somehow facilitate class discussion. Midterm is easy because all questions came from lecture notes, and final is in a similar manner. He is not very clear on paper/presentation requirements but overall he's easy & fair.

He is honestly one of the best profs in SFU. His lectures are very interesting and he always encouragae student to discuss with the class. He also introduces current global issues for student to talk about every lecture.
Good prof. His classes were very interesting and entertaining. He provides all direct and clear instructions to study for his exams.

Good teacher. Interesting and entertaining class. The group presentations were sometimes a bit of a drag. For exams study the notes and listen to what he says and you are gold. He knows his stuff and is enthusiastic to share it with us. finally love the motivational music before the exam starts.

He may be knowledgeable about the subject but he cannot teach it! His power point slides are all over the place and he doesn't use the textbook so there's no where else to look for information if his lectures are unclear. Just memorize the slides and you should be okay for the tests however I didn't learn anything valuable in this class.

If you're hesitating about your business major - take this course. Could be your turning point to true happiness. After taking BUS303 I finally realized how much I HATE business and all those waste-of-money classes like bus303. I've quit Beedie and I'm sooo happy I'm not gonna do any of the useless things anymore. Only design now, only real stuff.

Hope you like writing reflections because that's pretty much the entire course. Tom is a nice person however his class is not organized well. There is no clear syllabus or anything that shows what will be taught. He also makes online quizzes that only becomes available a few hours before class.

This guy had the hardest, most difficult quizzes. I walked into the course with an open mind, thinking that "Business Ethics" could actually be something to consider. However, the abysmal teaching methodology this nut bar follows has allowed me to conclude that business ethics is a waste of knowledge.

Incredible. Tom's methodology is different from most profs at SFU-his classes are all based on synthesis and understanding, not simple regurgitation. A lot of the negative comments on here come from students who are used to memorizing a textbook for an A. Tom makes you think and understand, and does it with a minimal workload. Simply a great prof.

Great teacher who is passionate about his subject, and provides useful assignments to emphasize course material. He is approachable and helpful with student questions. I would definitely take this class with him!
Such a bad proof! dont take it with him! so many assingments! avoid this class with this prof! take it with korth!
Great professor! learned alot and its one of those lecture you can just sit back and enjoy. Very understanding person and accomodating! i recommend this prof

Marks ridiculously hard!!! On every little thing!!! Inconsistent with his expectations. Million little assignments. Too bad it's a mandatory class for business students. Do not suggest taking it with him take it with someone else

She is a good prof. and encourages people to voice out their thoughts which adds an extra dimension to the lecture. Being said that one must read the weekly reading material to understand the lectures and take notes of the important concepts. It's a lecture heavy course and she doesn't share her ppts. Interesting class and relatively easy!

Pretty good prof. Has Interesting topics in class like world poverty, facial discrimination in the workplace, technology taking over etc... She does promote her vegan feminist views a lot, however, in a fair and intelligent manner. Exams were fair and grading on papers were fair too. Overall a solid prof
BEST CLASS AND PROF EVER!!! SO INSPIRATIONAL!!!! I LOVE YOU MICHAELA!!!!!

Her exams are heavily based on lecture notes. You need the textbook for her papers and to memorize quotes for exams. Her exams are fair; she provides a review guide which specifies concepts you should study. You will have to memorize a lot of information for her exams.
I love her lectures. One of the best profs I've had at SFU. Extremely engaging and intelligent. Course really opens your eyes up. Have to attend lectures. Highly recommend.

Lecture slides arent posted. But she tells you what you need to know in class, and gives amazing guidelines for her mid terms which allow you to pretty much learn all concepts. She is always willing to explain concepts or readings if you talk to her or even go to her office hours. Very compassionate person as well.

Very respectful, but the class can get heated. Often goes off on long tangents. Doesn't post slides so you need to attend class or get them elsewhere. Subjective marking and a lot of material to know. This isn't supposed to be a difficult course. My friends who had different profs were shocked at how hard we had it.

Michaela was an engaging lecturer but it seemed like she often used her lectures as a soapbox to promote veganism. She never showed up for her office hours. Both papers were graded subjectively by TA. Overall a decent class with decent amount of work (2 papers, midterm, final). Attend lectures for useful notes as she doesn't post them.
Michaela was a great prof overall and really encourages participation in lectures! She is quite open minded and allows students to disagree on reasonable matters.

Michaela was alright. She was very interested in what she was teaching, however she was also very opinionated and came off offensive to many people as a result. Her class had two papers, one MT and a final. Not too heavy of a workload. Papers and tests were not hard if you took time to review the material, but this also meant high averages.
Great prof and great class. Read the textbook and attend class for in class examples. Not a hard class but does require reading every week. Also get a good group for term project.
Really nice guy, 4 written reflections and a group project and essay. Midterm was easy if you go over his slides. Overall a super manageable course and high averages.
Honestly, not as bad as people make him out to be on ratemyprof. He's boring yes, but you get marked on a few things. Barely paid attention and managed to get a B-.
Barely went to class but kept up with the readings cause of the weekly assignments. Just know the important theories of each chapter final will emphasize the four theories of bus ethics during exams.

The lectures were fine, a little boring. A lot of the criteria and the grading was unclear, in my opinion. The biggest issue I had with the class was I made it a point to attend and participate in EVERY lecture, and somehow still only received 84 for the participation portion, which was still above the average, so I don't how I could've gotten 100.
His class is so damn boring that his lectures are like lullaby. He does not provide clear feedbacks with his reflections. He does not provide proper criteria for ethics individual assignment.
class is zzzzZZzzzzz, grading kind of weird, doesnt really provide feedback or context, no clear criteria. nice guy though.

Prepare for the deadly written reflections. Most of the reflection's required readings were 20 pages long and you have to read them in order to write the WR. TA will mark the WR and you are most likely going to get the same mark over the semester (low or high demand on you). Definitely avoid if you are not a patient reader or have a heavy schedule.

I really enjoyed taking BUS303 with Hooley! Requires some work (reflections, exams, participation, Ethics Project) but definitely manageable. Exams are all based on his slides, and he is a very fair and generous marker. If you're willing to learn the material, you will do very well in his class. Taking this course with Dan makes it worthwhile.

This class wasn't that hard but time consuming. The ethics project is fine if you get good group members but he decides the group by last name..... -__- very annoying. anyway his lectures are alright but kind boring at times.
Dan is great. Take him and you wont regret.

Dan was great, kept material interesting and relevant. He tests only on PPT slides so the textbooks and reading are unnecessary. Papers are graded fairly easy, and tests aren't difficult if you understand and memorize course content. Pay attention in lectures and make notes on PPT's and you'll be good. As good a choice as Shafik.
AVOID! I wish I took this class with Shafik instead of DAN. He could get through the lecture material in 1.5 hours but instead takes 3. VAGUE essay prompts. V boring.
One of the most boring and impractical business courses available at SFU. Avoid Dan, take it with Shafik instead, I didn't but I wish I did.

BUS303 is a full 3 hours of Hooley talking and the worst part is that he's a boring speaker. Going to lecture is really informative, but it's super boring. His slides are constructed really poorly and really text heavy.

Daniel is one of my favourite instructors at SFU. He is really helpful and provides good feedback on assignments. So if you cannot take 303 from Shafik don't worry since Daniel is as good as Shafik IMO. His lectures are chill but you have to participate in class discussions. His exams and assignments are fair. I definitely recommend him!
No Comments

He is absolutely THE BEST TEACHER I've had in business. If you are interested in the subject and listen to what he has to say in class, you'll have no problem with the exam. He is really smart and makes of his course more than another dry business-type empty talk. However, if you are the typical "business-type", you might hate him.

worst prof ever! the exams and assignments were unfair. we dont get part marks even with explanations. we have to get the answer write then he marks on explanations.  the scoring system intentionally makes students' marks lower. he doesnt tape the lectures, so going to class is mandatory to get the notes. not a very nice person to talk to either

You have no choice, I guess. It seems like he's the only one teaching this course. This is useless, at least i think, and boring class. He keeps talking in the class, so you gotta write down what's out of his mouth. each and every word! class average was so high, so you gotta do really hard. He's a good prof, but I think the class sucks.

Havers is the most entertaining and interesting prof I have ever had at SFU.  Exams are hard but worth the trade off of actually learning about the theoretical paradigms of business. Far more interesting than memorizing meaningless definitions and figures like other bus courses. He also has a wicked sense of humor!
No Comments

Absolutely the worst professor I've ever had.  The exams were unfair and his stubborn elitist nature was impossible to deal with.  Thank goodness they've finally added other professors to teach this course.  It wasn't even about "ethics in business", more of a philosophy course.  Avoid Havers at all cost.  You're welcome.

Generally an interesting prof and very good course content for future business people.  Good language skills are very important for this class, though. The readings use high-level language, and the exam expects you to be able to interpret abstract questions. Lastly - you might find him offensive if you are a conservative or republican.

Im confused, why did we buy the textbook?  It becomes apparent during lectures that he himself hasn't read it.  If you look at the page references he gives during lectures and then what he explains the concepts as, sometimes they are completely different.  If he finds your writing to be the least bit vague he will take marks off, because he can't b

well he tries to fail students during the finals.. i was getting B+ and A before finals, then during the finals, he tested stuff that he even didn't mention or appeared in the text books at all. clearly he wants to fail most students who doesn't attend to class or even the students who go to class EVERY TIME

Class was VERY interesting. And Havers lecturing style was also great. BUT TERRIBLE prof- he marks like a robot. Gives extremely subjective questions but will only accept set answers. In addition he stopped recording lectures becasue "people stopped showing up" but this was absolutley not ture, there is a full class every week. terrible prof.
the best prof i ever had! he is very clear about his lecture. but you have to study hard for it in order to get a B or A.. read his notes do his sample midterm. it is very useful!

Tricky class. Like everyone else says, don't waste money on the book. It is key to attend all the lectures, then listen to them again online. He's fair, but you're going to have to work hard. Take advantage of the fudge factor for participating. It helped me.
I enjoyed this class.  He is a good lecturer.
Good prof. Cases are tough but exams are fair. You must attend his lectures and listen to his recorded lecture. Don't buy the books. Useless. He really promotes thinking.
worst prof ever.

Go to his lectures and understand the concepts inside and out and you'll do really well in the course.  He explains things better than the textbook.  The M/C for the exams were a little tricky though.
if u want to fail, go ahead to take his class. the worst prof i ever met
A hard prof, uses the same joke for every single class, the markings are unfair specially for the cases. The worst teacher ever!!!!!!!!!!!!!
No Comments

He did a great job! I really loved the course and would love to have him as an instructor again. I got an A in the class and all I did was listening to the digital tapes. Listen to them 1-2 times and take detailed notes then you'll do well on exams.

This course is a trap. Both Textbooks are waste of time and money. You have to write down every single word he says in class cause it is probably a MCQ on MT/Final. He is very intelligent though. This course needs a major makeover.

Repetitive, boring lectures; Multiple Choice questions that are ambiguous and not based on text or lecture. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS UNLESS YOU HAVE TO. I'm not even certain the useless material teaches any ethics at all. In short, it should not be a required course, if taught at all.
lectures are interesting.  But the tests are really tricky.  You have to know the material front to back.  But master the sample final and you'll do well on the tests.
He is very ambiguous in his interpretations. Poor hand outs!!!
No Comments
He is such a good prof! just go to the lecture and study the lecture tape when there is an exam, then you will do fine. I strongly recommend that you take him!!!
Excellent prof! Lectures are fun and interesting. Fair grading system. Take him!
lecture note is not helpful if you dont understand it. exams were hard...

Three tips: (1)don't buy the txtbook, (2)take super detailed notes (don't have to go to lectures but listen to online lectures as many times as necessary), (3)try to link topics that don't seem to link intuitively (e.g. dirty hands to environment ethics). He is a very good and humourous lecturer but his exams Q's can be very counter-intuitive.
this class is a cinch. take GOOD notes and you'll be fine. don't buy the text, you wont need it
Clear, interesting, sometimes funny.

he's a great prof! he's really nice to his students and is fair in marking.  He really knows his stuff and his classes are very humurous! never bored in this 3 hr lecture!! glad he's teaching at SFU! just make sure u take good notes in class and think how the topics relate to each other! o yea...he looks like a cartoon but in a good way tho!!

Repeats the same examples over and over again in every single lecture. Every lecture you will hear him talk about Body Shop, Nike, Phillip Morris, leaky condos, and that mushroom thing. Try to have a look at him even if you're not in his class, 'cause it's hard to find a living Machiavellian (teaching an ethics course!) nowadays.
Good public speaker, exams are tricky
So good, so nice.  One of the best public speakers I've ever seen.  He has a good sense of humour too.

This XXXX guy is the worst prof ever! He super subjective! He never listen to student. You are going to get 0 if you write stuffs from the text book! He always say "I DON'T MAKE ANY ASSPUMTION!" while he assume students understand what he wants. Plus, you have to define every single word.

Great lecturer (one of best in SFU), makes 3 hr lectures interesting and amusing. Cases are tricky b/c the answers are rather subjective, u either get it or u don't. MT and Final are relatively easy as long as you understand his notes VERY well. DON'T buy the text - just take detailed lecture notes.
Ethical to make money with an ethics course? Skip this guy. Eventhough the notes make sense, his exams aren't fair and they are quite misguiding.
very interesting.  This man knows his stuff and it is great to see an insightful/critical mind such as his in the business faculty
Pretty nice guy and his lectures are interesting. The MC exams are really tough. The text is worthless. The cases are either right or wrong, no in between.
Marking scheme is totally random, its all or nothing. Likes very simplistic answers, don't bother trying to apply the actual material to the cases or you will get 0. Final was hard!
Funny + smart prof; he has great stories about cheaters who later complain publicly...
Biggest @#$#$%# I've ever come across at the university; don't bother with office hrs, won't help worth a damn.  Very machiavellian, I know, it's ironic for someone teaching ethics.:(
Interesting lectures, and a nice guy. But his exams don't make any sense. You are not allow to have an opinion in this class which makes this course pointless.
good sense of humour. knowledgaable and experienced. stick to his notes, dump the textbook. M/C are tricky with unusual vocabs in the questions. notes from lecture is very important.

He's kind of weird, reminds me of a talk show host, but overally nice guy. Don't buy the book, even though he tells you to do so. Exam multiple choice questions are hard, since there are more than one correct answer and you have to pick the most correct.

One of the best Profs at SFU. Wonderful sense of humor. Loved the class and learned a great deal. His Exams are a bit tricky so i suggest you study his notes like the back of your hand. Assignments were pretty straight forward.

He's not very helpful in office hours.  He likes saying "you tell me", or "it's in the textbook".  Becareful when answering essay questions in your exam. Screw up one of them, you lose 10%.  By the way you need 60% to pass the course.
Interesting and informative lectures. M/C in final exam are unreasonablely confusing==> still wondering how do one prepare for those kind of questions.
One of the best profs at sfu.  Good sense of humour and fun lectures.  No need to buy the text.
Teach in a very straight forward way.  His lecture notes help a lot.
Don't buy text, it was useless.  Exams/cases are based solely on lecture so don't miss them.  For cases/short answer: If your answer/interpretation is not the same as his, u get deducted severely.

bad prof. His assignments are unhelpful, absolutist and picky. You must think like him or you get a 0.  his final exam is a killer with multiple choice being the worst.  don't be misled by his in-class humour.
Be careful with the wording on essay questions in exams. One mistakes and it's 5% gone from your final percentage.
Gives me the creeps.  Reminds me of a slick used-car salesman.
do not buy the text. interesting prof, but very picky marking. hard to get high mark.
Grant is awesome... take good notes and you'll do fine
Textbook is not useful.  Prof is quite vague.  He likes to hide concepts with colorful language.
No Comments
The prof is nice...but this course is very hard.
No Comments
No Comments
A VERY GOOD TEACHER~ his classes are fun and enjoyable!!!~~
No Comments
His MCs are ridiculously ambiguous, vague, regurgitative...
No Comments
EXCELLENT Teacher. He checks this website also. hehehe ;) it was a pleasure!!
No Comments
No Comments
Don't read the text book. In fact, don't even buy it.
Great prof. Make sure he's teaching 303 or DONT take it. The book's author sucks
Best professor ever! Interesting, extremely intelligent and sexy!!!
No Comments
Nice and cute!
Overall, better make its point across then the author of the book haha
Looks like Chandler!
Guarantee to laugh once during his class.  Memorize his notes to do well.
No Comments
Grants exams are based off of his notes, so take good notes!!
He is really kind but he has a lot of work.

Tom assigns heavy weekly work: readings, discussion posts, quizzes, and journals. Ther are 3 reports, a midterm, a final, and 3 in-class assignments. A lot of your work is graded by AI, so it's easy to lose points. The content could be interesting, but disorganized slides and unclear lectures make the class disengaging and efforts feel unrewarded.

Im usually positive, but this is the worst class I've taken. Tom is kind but a terrible lecturer. We sit in silence for 10 minutes to "meditate" then write a weekly reflection. Weekly timed quizzes dont match the readings and are a guessing game of what would Tom say. Grading and lectures are also very unclear. Save yourself and take w another prof
awful. just awful.

Run.  Take any other professor than Thomas.  Be prepared to have disorganized slides.  Be prepared to hear lectures about his personal career and life and nothing else.  The material itself is not difficult whatsoever, but he makes it convoluted and confusing.

Thomas is a nice person but his class is useless, it's a group therapy self improvement course that talks about ethics in business occasionally. In our final report, only 1/3 was ethics, the rest was felt like a 10 year olds diary of what he wants to be when he grows up, and some self reflection about “moral foundation” values. Money well spent.

The biggest waste of time and money. When you think of business ethics you think maybe you could learn something interesting but this was not the case. Tom is delusional when it comes to the fact he believes world peace will come with meditation. This class should automatically be removed and tom should not be teaching a business course.

He's a nice prof, I liked his lectures. However, his class felt more like meditation-group-therapy than an actual business ethics class. I  do get the idea behind it, but as someone who was actually excited to take a real business ethics course, I found it to be misleading. I also dislike how missing 3 classes fails you, even being late is noted.

It seemed he used his platform as a business ethics professor to teach about his own worldviews rather than business ethics itself. Tom's entire course is an application of Meditation, Daoism, and mindfulness to ethical thinking, which is not the worst thing to learn, but rarely has anything to do with business ethics in general.

Took a whole ethics class, yet learned nothing about ethics. Class is entirely useless. Thomas is a nice guy but his classes are painstakingly boring. Trys to teach you his own fringe belief of what ethics should be instead of what is actually useful and true. Makes you meditate every class, and is incredibly unorganized.
Super boring lectures, bad presentations. However, the readings are interesting.

Not a typical business class, but sometimes change is good. Tom is a super sweet man, though his grading is always super vague. The class content was very simple, though there were many repetitive assignments that made the class dry. Lots of discussion which makes the class more interesting.

Thomas takes an abstract approach to ethics that many business students can't comprehend and are quick to hate on. If you come into his class with an open mind, you will understand his journal and meditation sessions are extremely beneficial to ethical decision-making.

Thomas definitely is passionate about his job and cares deeply about his students. However, he is unable to effectively organize and operate his lesson plans. His business classes utilize non-business activities such as meditation and "feeling" journals. None of these practices relate in the slightest to business. Big waste of time.

Tom believes business schools do not teach ethics in a way which prepares students for the real world. In an attempt to "change the way business is done" (his words from his PhD paper), he focuses on teaching students the importance of emotional intelligence and meditation. Ironic, as these have little to do with ethics.

I can not believe this is a business class. This is a psych class mixed with ethics and morals and emotions and a bunch of other stuff. This is not a business class. I wish SFU made this an elective for people trying to try new things out. Class was sooooo boring I can't believe I paid money for this class.

Tom has a sort of hippy-dippy approach to business ethics, focussing on personal ethics how to improve your EI and decision-making skills. He was one of the most engaging profs I've had at SFU, very passionate about the lecture content and does what he can to support the success of his students.
Nice guy, but expects way too much from students. Classes are dull and boring, attendance is mandatory, there are class presentations and unreasonable assignments especially the last one.

This is the most useless business course you will ever take. You are paying money to sit in a room and meditate. He is just going into random articles that he find and deems relevant. 2 8-10 pages assigned in the last two weeks of the course with little no information provided beforehand. SFU remove this course and let your students save money.

Tom is an extremely caring and respectful professor who cares about his student's wellbeing. He replies to emails promptly. However, his lectures are extremely dull and there is mediation and presentations every class. There are also 3 papers during the semester and they are 5-10 pages minimum. Get ready to write!

He cares about his student's success but his lectures are disorganized. Assignment due dates are not outlined in the so called "syllabus". He disrespects student's time. He assigned 3 papers (8-10 pages) to be completed within 4 weeks of each other at the end of the semester. Space them out over the course of the 12 weeks, not 4 weeks!! Stressful!

Thomas is a good professor in the sense that he cares about his students and wants them to do well. However, he is super disorganized with his lectures and goes off tangent a lot by sharing personal stories. Far too many useless assignments/journal entries to do and his exams are way more in-depth than what he goes over in the lectures.

He focused on helping you develop and finding who you are. Although there is lots of meditation and reflections about yourself, he does it for your own good. He wants to see you succeed and puts in the effort to do do. He doesn't teach ethics, but rather it's a journey about finding yourself. Definitely recommend Tom.
horrible class.. need to buy 3 textbooks, one of them being his which makes ABSOLUTELY NO SENSE. I need to meditate every 5 minutes because I feel the urge to rip this textbook apart if I don't.

Lectures are mandatory but completely useless. Meditation is required at the beginning of the class. Thomas is a nice person and means really well, but doesn't understand students and is unorganized with canvas.

Super easy professor, focuses the class more on yourself and personal development rather than actual business ethics. Compared to other professors he is a lot easier and the material is not as dry. No final with him and the midterm was really easy. Requires journal articles and what you have learnt from that week. Also makes you meditate in class.

His class was all about feelings and getting to know thyself. Barf. In-class meditations (good chance for nap). Weekly reflections/journal entries and online group discussions. If you miss any discussions, your marks for all the other ones you did gets lowered. You have to be good at explaining your deepest values in writing and with lots of detail
Good prof

Possibly the BEST prof at SFU! Quirky, fun, knowledgeable about things way beyond business ethics, and gives a lot of relevant info and stories. Very clear on what she wants from essays and assignments. Exams are easy, she practically gives you the answers.
Really cool guy. Put a lot of effort into this class. Winks like sarah palin. Am sure class will get better as he gathers more examples.
Skip the lectures and read the textbook. If you go to lectures you're wasting your time. Slides are absolutely useless. Really hard to stay awake (except for videos).
No Comments
No Comments

While I applaud his effort in making his own slides, they're useless unless you add onto them. Maybe next time he teaches this course he'll actually provide an outline for the group project instead of making it up as he goes along. He made the course much easier after the midterm: A 60% average increased to a 78% average after the final.

He does not teach BUSINESS ethics, he simply teaches ethics with SOME business examples.  I was thoroughly disappointed with the inconsistency of his marking. "Group" project and final exam were marked too leniently, and did not sufficiently provide a  somewhat concrete distribution of grades. Positives: lots of videos, great to keep you awake.
At the beginning it was a boring class, but later it became more interesting. He makes it seem much more interesting. Lecture notes make no sense without the lecture.

The course is ethics. It WILL be boring but he makes it bearable with stories. Notes make absolutely no sense on their own; they don't flow without lecture notes. Or just read the text since slides seem to made from the text. Marking is a bit tough but he's consistently harsh and it's on a curve. Good prof, light workload, lousy subject
No Comments

Worst class ever! She spends more than half of the class having only two groups present their “In the News” presentations and never actually teaches anything. Sara is always late to class as well and has pushed the start time from 9:30 a.m. to 10:00 a.m. I want to emphasise again you will never learn anything BECAUSE SHE NEVER TEACHES ANYTHING.

Sara is really well spoken and knows her material well. The readings she gave were actually insightful, and the exams were also based on them. Participation matters, and the classes go by fast. The class will make you think from a different perspective on things.

Attendance is not necessary but participation makes a huge bit out of the final grade so definitely go to class. No tutorials for this class but there seems to be a lot of emphasis on reading. LOTS of reading. She is a little messy but is kind and approachable. Class often gets moved to zoom at the last minute or starts late. Very chill overall.

Sara clearly cares about her students doing well, judging by the amount of work and reading.  Showing up and talking in class is a big part of your grade, so definitely go.  That said, class often starts late or gets cancelled, which can make the course feel less intense overall.
Cancelled class first few weeks. Unclear criteria with no feedback. Wouldn't reply to emails on time. Would give 0 if possible.
VERY unclear marking/criteria. assignments were handed back with no feedback, so didn't know how to improve. lectures are always disorganized.

Sara is an outstanding educator who genuinely cares about her students. Her lectures are fascinating and captivating, and they push you to reflect carefully about morality, the ego, and how it affects you and the rest of the world. She offers you high-quality criticism and all the tools you need to advance both academically and personally.
Extremely unorganized, often shows up to class late with little notice, ambiguous assignments and grading criteria. Sara is a nice person, but her teaching style seems very inexperienced.

Sara is a phenomenal professor that truly cares about her students. Her lectures are interesting and engaging and they force you to think deeply about ethics, the ego, and how it impacts you and the world. She provides quality feedback and gives you every resource to improve both as a student and on a personal level as an individual. Sara is a gem.

Absolutely the most lazy and boring professor I've ever had. Two group projects, two papers, and two evaluation on the two other groups. They were marked unfairly because he only accepts HIS answers. Just do yourself a favour and don't take this class with him.

There is so much to do in his class. 2 group presenations and 2 case studies and a final. His lectures are boring as he reads the slide for half the class and students presentations for the other half. He expects you to know everything for the final. Lecture slides and info from presentations which is unfair as most are poorly done.

Ethics is a boring class already, yet he made the class even more boring. There were so many things you needed to do: a case on a book or an article, a debate, evaluations on other groups, two individual assignments, and a final exam. Lectures slides were very wordy and he basically read off the slides. He used full 3 hours with a 10 mins break.

One of the most relaxed business profs i've ever had. He is nice and approachable, but always late by 20 minutes. It was alright for me since i get to sleep in for a few extra minutes. Not all readings are useful, focus more on his notes. His midterms deviate little from previous semesters and are straight from powerpoints.

Take a class with him! He's very nice and his exams and assignments are completely fair.  High overall class marks mean that the class will be scaled down.  Makes boring content interesting with random tangents and interesting side stories.  Focus on lecture material (don't bother with textbook) for 303.  I would love to take a class with him again

Really nice guy, he seems to genuinely care, unfortunately he also is a bit of a scatter brain and goes on many tangents, making the class hard to follow.  In my term he marked too easily and ended up having to curve everyone down drastically, a bit of a shock when 70ish becomes low C's and 90s are A-'s.
great prof. he's really enthusiastic about the material and tries hard to make a not-so-interesting class interesting.
Easy prof...boring, but relatively easy

This is surely one of the worst profs I have ever had. Once he finally obtained the teachers guide his midterms finally made sense. From then one it was simple. The format of his lecture notes is questionable. Severely boring. His favroite word: "um".

Please take ethics from someone else! He lectures' aren't clear & he makes totally off-topic jokes which are irrelevant to the course. In the end, nobody laughs and no point comes across. Midterms r hard n case studies r marked brutally hard. HE WILL get ur frustrated!

Jason Ho is an awesome prof! His lectures are very engaging and he is hilarious! He wants the best for his students and tries to give out as many A's as possible. Always available for help. Definitely take marketing with him!

Agree with everything 3/29/10 said. Very interactive class, participation marks mean you have to contribute at least once in a while. Expects you to read assigned case studies + text so it's a lot of reading. Pop quizzes were like roulette bc it rarely covered even half the readings. Take-home exams sound nice but he marks hard bc you get a week.

Andrew is an exceptional professor. I have never taken a more interactive 300-level class. Class discussions consist of real-life issues and raise your awareness level to the things around you greatly. He does his best to help students and is a truly caring guy. Take home MT and Final. Take this class with him!

Assigns book chapters + extra case studies for reading so dont take if you hate reading. Class is 1/2 open discussion, 1/2 lecture. Pop quizzes. Will mark hard & expect lots for take home exams cus you had lots of time to work on it. Overall a nice guy interested in his work but wasn't very good at giving direction for exams or group report.
Great prof-  He makes this class the easiest one you'll take in 3rd year.  3 hour lectures are a bit rough to sit through though.
Make sure to study his slides. His exams are based on those only. Don't even have to read the text! Easy class, nice prof :)
I like Bill! He is very good at explaining things and his slides lay everything out in a clear, easy to understand format. However, I did try to go to his office hours and he was a complete no-show!
Super boring! Doesn't give clear instructions on assignments.  Topics covered in class seem irrelevant to "business" ethics.  Not recommended.

I'm sure he's a great guy in person (seems genuinely nice and he cares). But as a professor - BOOOORING!!! Honestly, he will put you to sleep (especially since it's so bloody hot in there!). 3 hrs is way too long to sit through this guy. Discussion/readings are dull too. I want to learn about current ethics cases (ie. US banks), NOT first nations.

He's a great guy but very unclear on assignments.  You don't really know what is expected of you until you get your grades back and by then it's too late.  His pop quizzes which are just supposed to make sure you're attending class are way too difficult.  You have to read the book really carefully to be able to do well on them.
Participation required. (almost 30%) A 5-min skit worths for 20% of final grade. Extremely boring class! Do not recommend him!

Don't even consider taking this class unless you want to waste your tuition. It's the same things every week and somehow thinks her teaching is engaging by forcing participation. I don't see why SFU pays her. Meditation honestly causes more stress than relieves it (every week and mandatory). There are in class assignments too. Attendance mandatory

My dementia ridden grandmother could do a better job running this course. Made us meditate at the end of EVERY lecture and then made us reflect on the experience. Genuinely the worst experience I've had at SFU. Please, Neha, do us all a favour and retire.
There isn't a clear grading criterion; bless the TA for making things make sense. Also, I didn't love the mandatory meditation sessions; they felt fake, and I rarely relaxed during them.

What a nothing class, everyone just zones out on their computer or phone for 3 hours while she recycles the same junk she has been saying all term. Honestly, not a bad class if you just need to pass something by doing nothing, what a waste of money tho holy moly.

When asked to review previous quizzes and answer key/rubric BECAUSE THE FINAL IS ON THE PREVIOUS QUIZ CONTENT. She could not give a grading rubric, answer key, or even whats considered a correct answer cause a correct answer doesn't exist. Higher grade = Use pen because it makes your answers 'structured' Funny coming from least structured prof ever

Most unorganized & unhelpful prof I've ever had at Beedie. She said to review past quizzes to study for the final exam. We asked to see the past quiz questions & the correct answers from the quizzes & what response would receive points/a high mark. She said there's no 'correct' answer, she doesn't use a rubric, the 'rubric' is the question itself.

For an ethics professor, she is extremely condescending.  Will laugh at you if you ask for a marking rubric.  Will not explain how you are graded.  Whoever can write the most will get the best grade (regardless of content).  Lectures are disorganized.  Feedback is next to none.  Would never recommend this professor and would give a 0/5 if I could.

Unclear assignment marking guidelines, based off "vibes" and subjective. 
Refused to provide previous assignments for review, no practice exams
Performed well on all assignments, zero instruction on how to improve grades
Lecture slides were created by a highschooler. 
If you like receiving feedback and improving your marks, this is not the prof.
Her 5 star ratings are like the Trump administration's statements on the current status of the United States

99% sure this prof is one of the new DEI hires that SFU got recently. Grading is up to how she feels towards you or how she is that day. You learn more about ethics from the readings than what she teaches in class. You know it is bad when the TA crashes out in class and has to explain grading guidelines (A+ very useful, unlike the prof).
Completely arbitrary grading, refuses to provide correct responses for past assignments, refuses to provide marking rubric. I think she just makes stuff up tbh.

She does not give any concrete, actionable feedback and has been disrespectful. She has referred to students as "you people" and is inherently incapable of leading a class on ETHICS. Do not take this professor.

disorganized, unprepared lectures, inaccurate slides & readings, arbitrary grading. took marks off for not writing enough after warning us not to write too much. seemed confused about course content. the presentations overwhelmed her so gave us all same mark. had to remove final bc she messed that up too

She's really unfair about grading. She removed the final exam because she didn't check it before printing it out that the exam showed the correct answer in it. TRY TO AVOID HER AT ALL COST! Save your sanity, and yourself

Gave out the answer key as the final exam, so she had to remove the final from our grades completely. Feels unreasonable after I studied hard and was counting on a good final to make up for lower grades from her presentation debacle and other inconsistent marking. Not going to stop complaining about this professor for a while.

Constant rambling & tangents, both in lectures & in exams. She spent first 30 mins of 1 hr final interrupting with irrelevant info. Inconsistent marking and assignments that change at the last minute. Screwed up our presentations & then blamed us for it. My grade could literally be an A+ or a D, I have no idea what to expect. Avoid.

Unfair and arbitrary grading practices, doesn't follow the syllabus, disorganized slides and lectures, no respect for us as her students. She is reason SFU allows us to file for grade reviews. A joke of a professor.

Screwed up our major presentations so badly that she just gave up and gave us all the same mark. Exams were hard & tested obscure info hidden in the slides. Marking was confusing and inconsistent. My expectations are very low for my grade even though I attended every class and put in the work. NOT a gpa booster.

Inconsistent with her grading, disorganized and confused at many times. The 5 star reviews below are just a farce to create a falsified image she wants to have at SFU. Pissed off the the class last day by speed running through presentations maticulously planned by many. Horrible, immature, and unethical (and she teaches a lvl 3 ethics class)
The final exam is pretty much all MC, and the correct answer is lighter than other answers… I actually wonder if this is an ethical course

Very disorganized course with unfair grading, the difference between the highest and lowest mark on a skit worth 20% of our grade is less than 1 point. Final exam was unfair. Our final grades will probably be determined by rolling a dice.

An unethical prof for an ethics course. Cold, dismissive, disorganized and not knowledgeable about the course material. Handled our presentations in the most immature and foolish way possible. Made me feel more confused every time I asked a question. I was fooled by the 5 star reviews, but trust all the 1 stars and just take 303 with ANYONE else.
If Neha spent as much time preparing for her lectures as she clearly does writing 5 star reviews for herself, she wouldn't have to write 5 star reviews for herself.

She was extremely unprepared for all the lectures and I felt like she was learning some of the information along with us. Incapable of answering questions. Adds meditation to each class so she can spend less time teaching. Pushed all our presentations to the last day, and then rushed through them, which was frustrating and degrading. The worst.

Extremely disorganized with poor time management. All presentations were rushed. Class slides were useless and nonsensical - example: "How do we explain xyz? Answer - remember to always explain xyz". Midterm was all long answer and marked inconsistently. Final was all MC. She took an interesting class and turned it into a frustrating waste of time.

DO NOT TRUST THE 5 STAR REVIEWS. They are quite literally fake. You can see its the same 6 accounts disliking every 1 star review and liking every 5 star review. The most disorganized class I have ever witnessed.  She has no concept of time management. Take at your own risk.

No. No. No. Don't trust the 5-star ratings guys.. Easily the most disorganized and frustrating professor at SFU. She struggles to keep her schedule on track, and I find it very hard to follow her lectures. 3 assignments in ONE WEEK with presentations. I feel like she tries harder to keep her RMP ratings up than to keep up with her class.
Probably told the family dog to "hurry up" and make an RMP account to leave a good rating.

Take her class at your own risk. An ethics prof at SFU writing fake RMP reviews is just so ironic LOL. This is the only professor who spends more time writing herself good RMP reviews than actually planning and teaching the actual class. Horrible time management and dismissive towards students' presentations that took weeks to make.

Prof Neha grading is the most fare and impartial. She is very organized in her teaching style. I never faced any rush in her classroom. In fact she gives us extra time for us to meditate. She wants the class to feel less worried and stressed. She is very very polite in the whole university.  100 percent will recommend her.

The best part is professor is so calm. We feel very relaxed in her class. We meditate , enjoy every session and get inspired to become a better human being. In today's world when people are forgetting their core values it's nice to know professor gives huge emphasis on this and herself follows. Her own values are very high.

An amazingly understanding professor no reason to believe that she is not organized. I think she is the most organized professor in Beedie untill now. I have never seen anyone so sweet and super supportive like her. 

I will recommend her. Truly best teacher till now
She is the worst prof I have had throughout my uni years.

She ran out of time today and then rushed students, telling them to “hurry up” even while they handled technical issues. She shifted the fault onto the students instead of owning her poor planning, and even made people speed through or skip parts of their presentations, despite them being graded by the class.

This is a really poor experience for an ethics class, and I felt it strongly after today's lecture. She doesn't seem to know how to manage time, and most of the presentations ended up being pushed to the last class—even though her original plan showed they were supposed to be spread out weeks ago.

The most frustrating, disorganized professor I've had in Beedie so far. They struggle to keep track of their own plans, deadlines, and criteria. Condensed 3 weeks of presentations due to her poor time management and made dismissive, unhelpful comments. Horrible experience. Do not trust the 5-star ratings, I suspect the prof themself made them.

The professor was so dismissive and rude to students' feelings during presentations. Made us spend so much time making them, and then made us skip through them even though they were graded by our peers. Had 3 weeks of presentations scheduled and pushed them all into one day, and each presentation was supposed to be 10 minutes each. Rushed everyone.

Neha struggled to manage the class, making it hard to follow lessons. The final presentations were rushed into one day due to poor planning. She interrupted groups with dismissive comments, and technical issues went unresolved. Overall, the course felt disorganized and ineffective.

Highly unorganized. Midterm was 2 hours of nonstop writing. She skipped or rushed group presentations even though they're peer-graded. No assignments all semester, then suddenly three in one week. Lectures make no sense. TAKE IT WITH SOMEONE ELSE.

Her grading is unclear and subjective, we had nothing due all term and then she makes 3 things due on the same day, and she has absolutely no time management skills. She wants to make class engaging but just drones on. There are a bunch of reviews from this semester and I am willing to bet they are all fakes by the prof

I like her teaching style. I wait for her class every week to attend. Professor's class is a very relaxing experience. An exceptional teacher who is patient, approachable, and deeply invested in every student's growth. Always supportive, encouraging, and committed to creating a welcoming classroom.

Professor delivers high-quality instruction, maintains excellent classroom management, and uses innovative teaching strategies that meet the diverse needs of learners. Shows strong dedication, professionalism, and consistent excellence in student progress. I will 100 percent truly recommend her.
Nice course and a wonderful prof. Haven't had any issues at all.
Course integrates psychology and philosophy with business. It's well structured. Would certainly recommend her.
I am struggling to stay focused in class because of her teaching style

Doesn't seem like they know what they are doing, their instructions are extremely unclear. Claims to want to make class engaging without taking any active efforts to do so. Would not recommend taking this course with this professor.
Awesome teacher. Lovely person. Engaging classes. No need to buy books. All the content is available systematically. She shares slides as well.  Tons of scope to score good grades. Fair grader.

Wonderful prof who cares for students! Content is nicely arranged in canvas. Classes are chill with no pressures. Tests are well spread out. Accommodated my requests twice already giving her time. I like her classes.

Unnecessary course. Have to do peer evaluations every single week when there is nothing to evaluate on. Randomly changes due dates without notice making 3 major projects due on the same day. Overly stressful for no reason, prof's grading scale is arbitrary.

The class is poorly organized with unclear due dates and marking criteria. Students are expected to be engaged in class but it does not seem like there were efforts put in to make the lectures engaging in the first place. Everything in this class is done with maximum inefficiency to waste everyone's time.

Absolutely the worst class I have taken. She is the most disorganized prof I have taken across 5 years at SFU with over 20 professors. The rubric is not clear, her grading is abhorrent and participation is mandatory. I usually don't mind participation marks, but it serves absolutely no purpose in this class. Expect 3 hour dry boring lectures.

Most unorganized course/professor I've ever had to deal with. Expectations are not clear. No clear rubrics for short answer questions. Her marking seems to be based on feeling and is extremely subjective. Most of the topics discussed in class are not available on slides and the unorganized weekly readings are a must.

One of the most disorganized class I have ever take. Most of the topics discussed in class are not available on slides. The grading is extremely vague, and students are expected to sit through a 3 hour monologue on a weekly basis to learn ethic jargons. Please consider to take this class with another professor if possible.

One of the best classes! More learning with less stress for a theoretical course on Ethics. All the information was easily accessible. No need to buy books. Just be attentive and follow the guidelines. She is nice. You'll enjoy the skits.

genuinely one of the most disorganized classes i have ever taken, no clear grading, just zero vibes and running on some class activities. meditation is the only thing I learned from this course, GPA booster? lol.

Neha is very nice and always ready to support. I find her classes balanced with interactive lectures, group activities, 10 min meditation. As someone who struggles with anxiety, I was worried about taking a course on ethics but felt comfortable from the very first class with her. Overall, a life-transforming course for me. Attendance is required.

Neha was a nice professor, and she was very enthusiastic, but the class felt like a joke. Neha was quite disorganized and would often ramble about random ethics jargon. It never really feels like you are truly learning something useful. You don't need to study much in this class either, but participation is mandatory.

One of the more underrated professors from Beedie, assignments are fairly straightforward and be sure to keep up with weekly assignments, journaling and participation and you should be fine for the most part.

All you need to do is go to class, do the class, do mediation, write the weekly journals and nail the assessments. She's chill with deadlines but show her respect for her teaching and the you can easily score a B without having to overextend yourself. I personally took a lot away in terms of learning to be a better person overall.

Went to her first lecture today and she seems super sweet and understanding. Asked us to let her know if we want to change anything in the syllabus which is quite unheard of with most profs. Near the end of class we did 5 minutes of mindfulness/meditation. No midterm but there is a final. Assignments don't seem like they will be too hard!
Intelligent professor, fair with grading, popular yet so down to earth. Sadly, she does not teach other courses. I will miss her classes.

lectures are so dry and boring to survive through, and the prof is so disorganized. the way she words things is so confusing. the class itself is okay overall, its just weekly journals, a group skit, and a midterm/final. take it if you don't have any better choices
He is extremely chilled and easy going. Also a really nice guy. Class is too easy to be on a curve.

There really isn't anything to study in this course. Getting an B+/A- is incredibly easy, but doing any better than that seems impossible because of the curve. He has a dry sense of humour, which is at times funny, and other times rude.

Good lectures and funny prof, but be warned; he is ideologically possessed, very old school, rude when you talk to him or approach for questions. Take James Pflanz instead, at least he doesn't yell at me over the phone when i request office hours.

The professor i came across as an international student in canada. He cares for students and has a good sense of humour. I took this course bcoz i have intrest in law and the lectures were so intresting. Highly recommended without a doubt he is the best and knowledgeable.
Best professor in Beedie.

I took Bus 303 this semester with professor bhalloo and honestly all of the reviews on here are very accurate. I am not a morning person but still found myself attending his Friday morning lectures. He is super funny, approachable and uses real life scenarios to make the course engaging. 1 midterm, 1 final and a group project!

Shafik was a great prof that explained concepts clearly and did a really good job enforcing your learning. He covered topics in a fashion that keeps people entertained and interested. I really enjoyed his class!! There was no homework other than 1 group project (+ 1 midterm and 1 final), which means the workload is light (and they aren't hard too).

I like his style of teaching business ethics. I took both bus law and bus ethics with him and I. Both classes he was consistent. Very clear and easy to understand. I think the final was a bit difficult but he marked it very fairly . He genuinely wants you to succeed in class and is always available for help. My favourite teacher at Beedie

I was a little scared of taking this course but I had Shafiq for Bus Law An day loved it. What made this course excellently was his style of teaching and also his examples and how he interacted with us in class. Ed is re spectrum Andy very fair but also kind
Good teacher but lectures are a bit on the longer side sometimes. He could give a longer break in the class. Examples are very good but some examples a bit too long

Taking business ethics and also business law with him at SFU. His classes are always full, even the early morning one on Tuesday at 8:30 . He comes very prepared to perform. He is very good and has a way of making material interesting and enjoyable with real examples. He is always available to help, even okay taking calls at his office.

Shafiq is an ecellent prof. Very caring and smart. I took his business ethics class and found him very good at explaning difficult concepts with examples. Fair marker. He loves people participating and very good at getting people to participate. Very respectful. I am taking bus 393 with him in the fall. Just got in his class. Was on waitlist.

Amazing lectures, easy to understand, always prepared and very engaging. I will take him again in ethics course business 303. I wish he would soon teach employment Law course. My friend took his employment course and said he was excellent.

I had prof Shafiq for bus 303 and enjoyed it so much that I have him for BUS393. He make law and ethics very easy to understand and he has really good example to break things down in bite sizes to make you understand. His lectures are really good. I am there at 8:30 in the morning. Not many classes in the last 3 years I have woken up for at 8:30

Amazing lectures. He is excellent and easy to listen to and super smart. Knows his stuff. Had him in commercial law and then in business ethics course last summer. Would take any of his classes as he is my favourite prof at SFU Beedie. Very approachable and always available to you. Treats you like a human being.

I like Shafiks lecture, never skip one class and understand the basic concepts of bus 303. The grade is straight forward, one midterm and one final assignment, same format, very similar to the example midterm. Practice and ask him. Be free to provide own experience to him at last question. But the average is high, got 82%, and maybe around median

I like shafik because his lectures are very clear and he knows what he is talking about. Gives real world examples to make his point. He makes boring stuff exciting. He is honestly interested interested in making students comfortable and encourages us to participate in class. In the 3 years at school, I have to say he is the best prof I have had.

Taking both his courses this semester: business 393 commercial law and business 303 ethics. He is the best prof to take in both courses but it is difficult to get into his class. I got in last minute when the department opened the course and allowed some of the people on his huge waitlist to register. You have to take him.

He makes everything very simple and straightforward. 1 midterm, 1 group assignment, 1 final with SAME FORMAT! He explains the theories over and over + also breaks down structure of assignments with crystal clarity. If you didn't get a high mark in this class, then something's wrong with you not him. I couldn't have asked for a better prof for 303.

Heard that ethics is confusing and hard to get good grades before taking. However, Shafik does his best to make the class so straightforward and easy to follow. No need to read the book as his lecture notes included everything. It is good to attend classes to clarify confusions and listen to his examples. One of my best prof at SFU :)

303 is pretty straightforward. Teaching style is relaxed and friendly. Is always super open to students reaching out to him for questions. Pretty subjective class. 80% average on MT and 80% on assignment. Hard to get a high mark due to high curve.

I laughed, I cried, I laughed again.  That has nothing to do with the class, I'm just bipolar. Shafik is terrific, his skill at making a point clear through his stories is awesome. He made sure to get to know almost everyone and we all felt more involved because of it.

Totally awesome prof. I find his lectures very informative and so much practical info. It helps being able to lecture like he does. He is an excellent story teller and tons of examples to make you understand materials. Definitely most interesting class I have had.

I too 393 and 303 with him and found him equally excellent in both classes. He is very clear and like all here his examples are very good. If you did not do well it is because you are lazy or not smart. He goes out of his way to help you. Excellent teach for sure.
